description_long: This object is a large, medieval-style wooden catapult mounted on four spoked wheels, constructed primarily from dark timber beams reinforced with metal bands. The frame is rectangular and robust, with diagonal supports and a central pivoting arm that features a large, spoon-shaped cup for launching projectiles. A thick rope is strung across the front, connecting to the throwing arm, which is tensioned by a winding mechanism at the rear. The wheels are solid and wooden, designed for rolling the device across flat surfaces. The overall build is heavy and utilitarian, with exposed mechanical components and a functional, rugged appearance.
description: A large, wheeled wooden catapult with a spoon-shaped launching arm and reinforced frame, featuring visible ropes and metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide crossbar, taut rope, and the rounded end of the throwing arm centered above the frame.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the angled throwing arm, spoon-shaped cup, and the full length of the rectangular wooden chassis with four wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the winding mechanism, the back of the throwing arm, and the structural supports within the frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the angled arm, cup, and the arrangement of the wheels and support beams.
